channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities
item.description:
Georgia lawmakers have drafted a pair of bills that are set to transform the way math is taught in the state. If the bills become law, they’ll open doors for students to receive a high-quality math education and ultimately shape the direction of their lives.

The post Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story
item.description:
RGF has been very active in opposing the plan to enact a massive gross receipts tax hike at Albuquerque City Council. You can find out more about the plan here and even use the links in the post to reach out to your council members. RGF president Paul Gessing recently sat down with KOAT 7 […]

The post RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] NJ ED REPORT: As Other States Move Forward, Will New Jersey Leave Education Dollars Behind?
item.description:
Wells Winegar is Executive Director of the New Jersey Policy Institute, As Governor Mikie Sherrill begins her term in office, one of […]

The post NJ ED REPORT: As Other States Move Forward, Will New Jersey Leave Education Dollars Behind? first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] INTERVIEW: NJPI Chairman Discusses the NJ Education Landscape on New Jersey Politics with Laura Jones
item.description:
Check out NJPI Chairman Andy Mulvihill as he is interviewed by Laura Jones on the landscape of K-12 education in […]

The post INTERVIEW: NJPI Chairman Discusses the NJ Education Landscape on New Jersey Politics with Laura Jones first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] The Schoolhouse as Clinic: The Dangerous Mission Creep of Public School Mental Health Screenings
item.description:
Virginia parents should be alarmed that the Virginia General Assembly just passed House Bill 355  which mandates annual mental health screenings for all public school students in grades 6 through 12. While framed as a compassionate response to a very real “mental health crisis,” it represents a fundamental shift in the mission of public education: from academic instruction to clinical surveillance.  By institutionalizing mandatory mental health screenings, HB 355 threatens […]

The post The Schoolhouse as Clinic: The Dangerous Mission Creep of Public School Mental Health Screenings  appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] New Jersey Policy Institute Announces Dr. Christopher Young to Board of Directors
item.description:
Parsippany, NJ — The New Jersey Policy Institute (NJPI) today announced that Dr. Christopher Young, an economist and Principal at […]

The post New Jersey Policy Institute Announces Dr. Christopher Young to Board of Directors first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill
item.description:
State legislators are paving the way for children to overcome challenges with literacy, empowering them to thrive in their academic development and build flourishing lives for themselves and their families in the future.

The post Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] Governor Spanberger: Don’t Make Principals Jump Through Hoops to Keep Students and Teachers Safe
item.description:
By Ali Ahmad and Derrick A. Max  Conservatives across the country are salivating at the idea that Virginia’s new governor may sign a bill that opens her to the charge that she is siding with school bullies and delinquents over teachers and principals.  House Bill 298, patroned by Delegate Delores McQuinn, is now on Governor Spanberger’s desk. […]

The post Governor Spanberger: Don’t Make Principals Jump Through Hoops to Keep Students and Teachers Safe   appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH ranks first in taxpayer ROI for 11th straight year
item.description:
New Hampshire ranked No. 1 in taxpayer return on investment (ROI) for the 11th year in a row, according to financial services website WalletHub.  “New Hampshire is the state with the best taxpayer return on investment, which is due in large part to the fact that it has no state income tax,” WalletHub’s report concludes. […]

The post NH ranks first in taxpayer ROI for 11th straight year appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Nebraska State Treasurer Joey Spellerberg. During the episode Treasurer Spellerberg and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss LB 938, which creates First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska, the role of the Treasurer’s Office, and solutions for Nebraska’s high property taxes. Listen now on Apple...

The post Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: A Step Forward on Healthcare Reform in Mississippi
item.description:
After a session filled with difficult setbacks and stalled reforms, this week brought a meaningful — and important — step forward. On March 23, HB 1622 was signed into law by the Governor, marking another shift in how Mississippi approaches healthcare regulation. While it is not full repeal of Certificate of Need (CON) laws, it […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: A Step Forward on Healthcare Reform in Mississippi appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] The Wisconsin experiment in economic freedom
item.description:
Evidence from border counties indicates increased economic freedom encourages prosperity for Wisconsin residents.

The post The Wisconsin experiment in economic freedom appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Liberating the Legal System: Beacon Urges Better Access to Justice
item.description: Tennessee has a choice: maintain outdated rules that restrict opportunity and choice or embrace reforms that respect individual rights, encourage productive achievement, and expand access to justice.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 12
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 12 In this Conference Weekend Edition of Capitol Chat, we break down key dates and legislative processes for the remaining 9 days of the 2026 Session. We also dive into the latest on teacher pay raises, PBM reform, a controversial veto, and banning lab-grown milk.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 12 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] There’s a simple solution to passing an assessment limit if legislators really want to help taxpayers
item.description: The Kansas Legislature concluded its work of the regular session last week amid considerable controversy over an assessment limit that would protect people from unaffordable property tax increases resulting from appraisal increases of 20%, 30%, or even higher. Earlier in the session, the Senate passed a proposal establishing a 3% annual assessment limit, with a […]

item.title: [Will Law] Evers Vetoes Bipartisan, Pro-Educator Legislation to Encourage Safe Classrooms
item.description:
WILL issued a statement in response to Governor Evers veto of AB 614 / SB 611, known as the Teacher Bill of Rights. The bill would have strengthened teachers’ authority to manage their classrooms, required parental notification when serious classroom incidents occur, and established clearer accountability so teachers can focus on teaching.

The post Evers Vetoes Bipartisan, Pro-Educator Legislation to Encourage Safe Classrooms appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Why Free-Market Capitalism Best Serves Kansas
item.description: Kansas does not need a new economic experiment. It needs more of what actually works. Free-market capitalism remains the best system ever discovered for human flourishing because it respects people as decision-makers, not as subjects of political management. It is built on private property, voluntary exchange, competition, and the rule of law. Those may sound […]

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Reacts to the Bothfield Redistricting Case Dismissal
item.description:
WILL praised a decision by a circuit court to dismiss a lawsuit against Wisconsin’s current congressional Maps. WILL filed motions to intervene in two lawsuits seeking to overturn Wisconsin’s Congressional Maps. Seeking again to defend those maps on behalf of a group of Wisconsin voters.

The post WILL Reacts to the Bothfield Redistricting Case Dismissal appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Empowerms] Dr. Jennifer Doleac: What Actually Deters Crime?
item.description:
Dr. Jennifer Doleac: What Actually Deters Crime? Public safety is foundational to strong communities, but too often, the policies we rely on to reduce crime are driven more by intuition than evidence. As Mississippi and states across the country look for ways to build safer neighborhoods, it’s worth asking a simple question: what actually works?…

The post Dr. Jennifer Doleac: What Actually Deters Crime? first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Beacontn] ExoticRidge v. Hawkins County: Fighting back against an arbitrary data center ban in East Tennessee
item.description: Executive Summary Technology is evolving like never before. Americans today benefit from rapid advancements in fields like artificial intelligence. These cloud-based advancements have a physical component: data centers. The data center industry has already generated trillions of dollars for the U.S. economy. And with companies projecting trillions of dollars more in investments to keep up...

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as host Jim Vokal rants about rising property taxes, the shell game elected officials are playing, and solutions Platte is advocating for this year. Listen now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

The post Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH’s rental housing is significantly older than its owner-occupied housing
item.description:
As America prepares to celebrate its 250th birthday, half of New Hampshire renters live in units built before the country celebrated its 200th birthday.  The median renter-occupied housing unit in New Hampshire was built in 1975, making it 51 years old this year, according to U.S. Census Bureau data.  By contrast, the median renter-occupied unit […]

The post NH’s rental housing is significantly older than its owner-occupied housing appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Federal Options for Large Scale Seawater Desalination
item.description:
The Carlsbad desalination plant is reportedly operating at half-capacity, basically because it’s less expensive to import water from the Colorado River. That is likely to change, as Arizona’s Department of Water Resources is negotiating with the San Diego County Water Authority to purchase some of the water they get from the Colorado River. The funds from Arizona will...

The post Federal Options for Large Scale Seawater Desalination first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] What’s Really in the Governor’s Final Supplemental Budget Package
item.description:
Supplemental budgets are often framed as routine housekeeping, minor adjustments to keep state finances on track. The Governor’s 2026 supplemental, however, was anything but routine. It represented a troubling fiscal expansion that risked driving up costs for Maine families and taxpayers. The Governor did not stop there, however, and has since released a Change Package […]

The post What’s Really in the Governor’s Final Supplemental Budget Package appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Senate Bill 1454 — Approp, H&W, ELD, youth, fam, add’l (-1)
item.description: Note: This year IFF rated maintenance bills according to a more refined system. This is an enhancement bill, and will be rated as a standalone bill. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. Bill Description: Senate […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 978 — Approp, H&W, pub hlth srvs, add’l (-2)
item.description: Note: This year IFF rated maintenance bills according to a more refined system. This is an enhancement bill, and will be rated as a standalone bill. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. Bill Description: House […]

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Free Money Trap: How Laundered Compassion Fuels a Culture of Abuse and Fraud
item.description:
The headlines are, by now, a wearying routine. In 2025, a federal takedown revealed $14.6 billion in Medicare fraud, involving kickbacks for allegedly unnecessary tests. In Minnesota, the Feeding our Future scandal saw $300 million vanish from a pandemic meal program, while childcare fraud drained another $100 million via ghost students. California, meanwhile, became the...

The post The Free Money Trap: How Laundered Compassion Fuels a Culture of Abuse and Fraud first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: The Final Days - and What Could Come Next
item.description:
We are officially in the final stretch of the legislative session. Sine Die — the last day of the regular session — is set for April 5. That means lawmakers have just days left to finalize remaining legislation, negotiate the state budget, and bring this year’s work to a close. But as things stand right […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: The Final Days - and What Could Come Next appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] West Virginia Enacts Right to Try for Individualized Treatments
item.description:
In a life-affirming victory for rare disease patients, West Virginia became the 17th state to enact the Goldwater Institute’s Right..

The post West Virginia Enacts Right to Try for Individualized Treatments appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana’s Nursing Home Moratorium Is Protectionism
item.description:
When the government makes it illegal to open new nursing homes or add beds where people need care, that is not health policy. It is protectionism.  And in Louisiana, HB 199 would keep that protectionism in place for years longer by extending the state’s moratorium on new nursing facilities and additional beds. Supporters may call...

The post Louisiana’s Nursing Home Moratorium Is Protectionism appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] In memory of Tom Howatt, embodiment of American Dream
item.description:
Tom Howatt, who rose up as the son of a Scottish immigrant to become President and CEO of Wausau Paper, a preeminent leader of Wisconsin’s business community and an exceedingly generous philanthropist, has died at the age of 76 after a two-year battle with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). Howatt, who was also a long-time chairman [...]

The post In memory of Tom Howatt, embodiment of American Dream appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Forty-year-old vehicle emissions program under new scrutiny
item.description:
Momentum is growing to end vehicle emissions testing programs in several states, including Wisconsin.

The post Forty-year-old vehicle emissions program under new scrutiny appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Albany Wavers on Shutting Down a Medicaid Racket
item.description:
As Washington threatens to crack down on fraud and abuse in New York’s Medicaid program, state legislators are doing their....

The post Albany Wavers on Shutting Down a Medicaid Racket appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Idaho Legislature Sends Bill Ending Taxpayer Support For Teachers’ Unions to Governor Little
item.description:
The Idaho Legislature this week gave final approval to Freedom Foundation-supported legislation that would end taxpayer support for teachers' unions in Idaho.

The post Idaho Legislature Sends Bill Ending Taxpayer Support For Teachers’ Unions to Governor Little appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] The Massachusetts Economy and the Inflation Reduction Act’s Medicine Price Controls
item.description:
Questions abound about the resilience of the Massachusetts economy.  Pioneer Institute has documented that high-net-worth individuals are fleeing the state for states with friendlier tax environments.  There is also a question about whether small businesses in the state can sustain and thrive in a business environment increasingly characterized by high...

The post The Massachusetts Economy and the Inflation Reduction Act’s Medicine Price Controls  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Courting Disaster: What Do New Social Media Rulings Mean for the Future of Free Speech and American Innovation?
item.description:
Last week, juries in New Mexico and California reached verdicts against Meta and YouTube of $375 million in civil penalties and $6 million for an individual plaintiff, respectively. These rulings are a harbinger of legal action to come, wherein states and individuals take to the courts in response to social problems they associate with social...

The post Courting Disaster: What Do New Social Media Rulings Mean for the Future of Free Speech and American Innovation? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Week in Review: 17 States!
item.description:
Patients in West Virginia with rare and ultra-rare diseases have a new reason for hope. That’s because Gov. Patrick Morrisey..

The post Week in Review: 17 States! appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 13
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 13 The 2026 legislative session is over… or is it? We break down the budget, education legislation, and a host of other bills that made it across the finish line during a busy week at the Capitol. 

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 13 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Homeschool Advocates Seek Answers After Committee Vote Reversal
item.description:
At least four Connecticut legislators changed their votes on a homeschool regulation bill on March 18, switching from “no” to “yes”, reversing the outcome and allowing the measure to advance […]

The post Homeschool Advocates Seek Answers After Committee Vote Reversal  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget
item.description:
Over the last several months, Maine Policy Institute has repeatedly pointed out the many problems that exist in Governor Mills’ 2026 supplemental budget. Maine lawmakers had an opportunity to fix serious flaws in the budget. Instead, the Legislature’s Appropriations Committee has taken an already problematic proposal from Janet Mills, doubled down, and made it worse. […]

The post From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Crucial Homelessness Accountability Bill Headed to Georgia Governor
item.description:
This week, Georgia lawmakers voted to hold local governments accountable when their officials allow homelessness to grow unchecked and harm..

The post Crucial Homelessness Accountability Bill Headed to Georgia Governor appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Ranked Choice Voting the Wrong Approach
item.description:
The following appeared in the Albuquerque Journal on April 4, 2026 Albuquerque’s “progressives” are at it again. Ever since Republican RJ Berry won a three-way election for mayor in 2009 (against two Democrats), Albuquerque has had runoff elections requiring candidates to achieve more than 50% of the vote. These runoffs can be costly. The city […]

The post Opinion piece: Ranked Choice Voting the Wrong Approach appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Lawmakers Are Settling for Average While Other States Win
item.description: Kansas ranks 23rd on tax competitiveness. In a race where people vote with their feet, that is not something to be proud of. Texas has no personal income tax. Florida has no personal income tax. Tennessee eliminated its tax on investment income. North Carolina has spent years cutting rates and climbing the State Tax Competitiveness […]

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Let Connecticut Keep Opportunity at Home
item.description:
Connecticut has a rare opportunity in front of it. The question is straightforward: Will we allow new investment in our students to stay here, or will we watch it flow to other […]

The post Let Connecticut Keep Opportunity at Home appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Wall Street Journal: Even Southern States Should Worry About Migration to Florida
item.description:
At first blush, there’s little reason for state legislators in Mississippi and Georgia to care about James Quigley’s cross-country...

The post Wall Street Journal: Even Southern States Should Worry About Migration to Florida appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant
item.description:
The following appeared on April 5, 2026 in the Santa Fe New Mexican. The article also appeared in papers across New Mexico. I recently came across a concept called the Curley Effect. It has nothing to do with Larry and Moe. After some research, I learned the Curley Effect, coined by economists Edward L. Glaeser […]

The post Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Who is winning the race for affordable power?
item.description: Despite a massive hydroelectric advantage, Washington will continue to become more expensive due to its energy tax and regulatory policies, while Idaho, Montana, and Wyoming are likely to see the cost curve bend with their support for bringing more nuclear power online.

item.title: [Will Law] Wisconsin Parents Underestimate or Don’t Fully Know School Funding Levels, New Survey Shows
item.description:
WILL, in partnership with 50CAN, a national nonpartisan education reform nonprofit, has released a new survey of Wisconsin parents. The findings reveal continued gaps in parental awareness of school spending, some improvement in knowledge of educational options, but a surprising gap in “workforce confidence.”

The post Wisconsin Parents Underestimate or Don’t Fully Know School Funding Levels, New Survey Shows appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Orlando Sentinel: Florida Isn’t Polarized. It’s Disappointed
item.description:
Look at what’s sitting underneath the poll’s headline findings. Seventy-one percent of likely Florida voters polled say the...

The post Orlando Sentinel: Florida Isn’t Polarized. It’s Disappointed appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG: DPI Teacher Report Requires Smart Solutions, Not More Spending
item.description:
IRG: DPI Teacher Report Requires Smart Solutions, Not More Spending Failure to train, staff, and retain better is creating a crisis for schools Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) analyzed the 2024 Educator Preparation Program and Workforce Analysis Report released by the Department of Public Instruction on Monday. DPI’s report says Wisconsin […]

The post IRG: DPI Teacher Report Requires Smart Solutions, Not More Spending appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Empire Center Breaks Down Albany’s Pork Barrel Spending
item.description:
Albany legislators steered over $83 million in grants to 293 local projects between April and December 2025 , according to....

The post Empire Center Breaks Down Albany’s Pork Barrel Spending appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Looking at Missouri’s “A” Districts
item.description:
Listen to this article While the Missouri Legislature continues to debate A–F school report cards, the Show-Me Institute recently released our annual report card update on MOSchoolRankings.org. Our rankings are […]

The post Looking at Missouri’s “A” Districts appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Fox 11: New data shows drop in Wisconsin educator retention, roundtable in Green Bay Monday
item.description:
(WLUK) — The Wisconsin Department of Public Instruction says it needs to strengthen educator recruitment and retention to address the state’s teacher shortage. That’s according to new data released Monday morning by the DPI Educator Preparation Program and Workforce Analysis Report. A discussion with local and state education leaders happened at the Green Bay Area Public […]

The post Fox 11: New data shows drop in Wisconsin educator retention, roundtable in Green Bay Monday appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] St. Louis County Should Reduce Plumbing Licensing Rules
item.description:
St. Louis–area plumbing companies are facing a shortage of workers (which is a nationwide trend), and they would like to reduce the county’s strict plumbing licensing system in order to […]

The post St. Louis County Should Reduce Plumbing Licensing Rules appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Has Taken an Important Step on Government AI—But the Commonwealth Must Do More to Improve Services, Transparency, and Save Taxpayer Dollars
item.description:
New Pioneer brief finds Massachusetts has reported just 20 AI use cases, only three of them directly serving constituents. 

The post Massachusetts Has Taken an Important Step on Government AI—But the Commonwealth Must Do More to Improve Services, Transparency, and Save Taxpayer Dollars appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] More legislation doesn't mean better governance
item.description: This year, Idaho lawmakers introduced more than 800 pieces of legislation—again. That’s not a one-off spike. It’s part of a clear trend: more bills, more activity, and more output from a citizen legislature that was never designed to operate at this scale.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Bears deal will be a top priority for lawmakers upon return
item.description:
Legislators face enormous pressure to finalize a megaprojects bill that would spell disaster for taxpayers.

The post Bears deal will be a top priority for lawmakers upon return appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] Is Union “Dues Skim” Coming to Virginia?
item.description:
There are many reasons why Governor Abigail Spanberger should veto the collective bargaining bill headed to her desk, a bill requiring local and state governments to bargain with union bosses even if less than a majority of public employees want the union or the bargaining. There is the fact that it will force major spending […]

The post Is Union “Dues Skim” Coming to Virginia? appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mackinac] High gas prices don’t justify emergency powers
item.description: Whitmer stretches executive authority again

item.title: [Jamesmadison] A Rare Reform Opportunity in the Highway Bill
item.description:
Surface transportation re-authorization gives Congress a timely opportunity to confront lawsuit abuse and transportation affordability Congress is preparing...

The post A Rare Reform Opportunity in the Highway Bill appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Hijacked: How the Bond Process is Being Used Against Taxpayers
item.description:
School bonds are one of the main instruments contributing to Texas’s local government debt, which is currently over half a trillion dollars. Key points: As of 2026, school bond debt in Texas is over $236 billion. In May of 2023, Texas passed 79% of the 245 bond proposals put on the ballot, costing taxpayers $22.9...

The post Hijacked: How the Bond Process is Being Used Against Taxpayers first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Mocking Motherhood, Missing the Point
item.description:
The mean girls are at it again. The ladies of “The View” reacted this week to a clip of Isabel Brown speaking in favor of marriage and children at the Conservative Political Action Conference. In the clip, Isabel says, “It is these choices, like deleting our dating apps, quitting birth control pills, and saying ‘I...

The post Mocking Motherhood, Missing the Point first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] What Comes Next In Operation Epic Fury: By Sea And By Land, Watch The Targets Closely
item.description:
President Trump’s remarks on Monday included both negotiations and a threat to hit dual-use infrastructure. In the days ahead, the direction of Operation Epic Fury will become unmistakable in the targets the United States and Israel choose to strike. The U.S.-Israeli air campaign has already delivered historic results. Our losses to enemy fire have been...

The post What Comes Next In Operation Epic Fury: By Sea And By Land, Watch The Targets Closely first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Southern Resurgence - What the Numbers Tell Us
item.description:
Something significant is happening in America's South, and it deserves more attention. While New York and California are losing residents, states like South Carolina and Alabama are gaining population at a record pace - and alongside that growth, it is southern states like ours that are generating some of the most impressive economic numbers in the […]

The post The Southern Resurgence - What the Numbers Tell Us appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] WALZ’S Education GRIFT: Grad Rates UP, Test Scores DOWN
item.description:
💭Did Walz improve Minnesota schools as Governor, or only make them worse? Comment below💭 Check out the American Experiment Podcast! Minnesota’s boasting a record-high graduation rate, but there’s more to this story than meets the eye, so Bill Walsh joins the show to shed light on Walz’s talking point (ft. STOP THE TAPE!). A convicted...

The post WALZ’S Education GRIFT: Grad Rates UP, Test Scores DOWN appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Foropportunity] GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act
item.description:
The Stronger Workforce for America Act of 2026 includes an invaluable option for state flexibility, the Make America Skilled Again Grants. Georgia and other states need this option so they can design workforce and safety net systems that meet families' immediate needs while providing a clearer path to opportunity and self-sufficiency.

The post GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG’s Quinton Klabon joins the Vicki McKenna Show
item.description:
What’s really behind Wisconsin’s teacher shortage and declining outcomes? Quinton Klabon of the Institute for Reforming Government discusses teacher training gaps, reading proficiency issues, and the debate over school funding and reform. Listen to the interview here.   

The post IRG’s Quinton Klabon joins the Vicki McKenna Show appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Starving Coal of Capital Puts the Power Grid at Risk
item.description:
Winter Storm Fern was a warning. When temperatures plunged into the single digits and heavy snow blanketed much of the country, the electric grid faced a serious stress test. For years, I have cautioned that rising electricity demand and the premature retirement of dependable power plants were putting the U.S. on a collision course with […]

The post Starving Coal of Capital Puts the Power Grid at Risk appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] WASHINGTON: It’s about giving workers options
item.description:
Public sector unions are not just workplace representatives; they’re powerful political actors with influence that extends across ballot initiatives, elected officials, nonprofit organizations and super PACs. 

The post WASHINGTON: It’s about giving workers options appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Energy Reliability is Only as Strong as its Weakest Wire
item.description: The Volunteer State is building a serious energy future that will continue to anchor our leadership in the sector. Allowing competition and technology to improve our state's grid will not only make the state more attractive for investment but also keep more Tennesseans safe when it matters most.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Should Minnesota expand free college?
item.description:
On Tuesday afternoon, the Senate Higher Education Committee heard SF 3754 and SF 4692 — two bills that would expand free college education in Minnesota. SF 3754 would extend eligibility for the North Star Promise — a program created in 2023 to provide extra assistance with tuition and other higher education expenses — to students with family incomes...

The post Should Minnesota expand free college? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Mackinac] Time to make full use of the new earmarks process
item.description: It’s OK to acknowledge that district grants are local

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Waste Watch: Illinois gives $500,000 to health advocacy non-profit
item.description:
A $500,000 grant from the state highlights issues in transparency and last-minute funding.

The post Waste Watch: Illinois gives $500,000 to health advocacy non-profit appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Run the Numbers: What Can Data Tell Us About AI?
item.description:
The conversation around artificial intelligence (AI) has quickly become a testing ground for competing philosophies, approaches to governance, and attitudes about progress and innovation. With both the technology and the debate becoming nearly ubiquitous, recent and strong data about the state of AI can help clarify how people are using it and the impact it is...

The post Run the Numbers: What Can Data Tell Us About AI? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome William Beard, co-author of the Goldwater Institute’s report: Data Centers a Free Market Model for the Digital Future. During the episode William and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss what data centers are, why they matter, and what separates the states that are winning investment...

The post Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Several MSPC policy recommendations were enacted this year
item.description: Lawmakers across our region were very busy during the 2026 Legislative Sessions. More than 2,300 combined bills were introduced in Washington (1,157), Idaho (817), and Wyoming (335). There wasn’t a legislative session this year in Montana. Several of Mountain States Policy Center’s (MSPC) recommendations were among those to make it across the finish line.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Grading Missouri Schools with Susan Pendergrass and Avery Frank
item.description:
Visit the site: moschoolrankings.org/ Susan Pendergrass and Avery Frank join Zach Lawhorn to discuss MOSchoolRankings.org, the Show-Me Institute’s website that assigns letter grades and GPAs to Missouri schools and districts using […]

The post Grading Missouri Schools with Susan Pendergrass and Avery Frank appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution
item.description:
The Maine Supreme Judicial Court has issued a unanimous advisory opinion in which the Court concluded that the Legislature’s attempt to expand ranked-choice voting (RCV) to general elections for governor, state senator, and state representative would violate the Maine Constitution.  Although the decision came quickly, the outcome closely tracks the concerns that surfaced during oral […]

The post Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] CALIFORNIA: Union hubris in any language
item.description:
After Freedom Foundation’s National Canvassing Director Dustin James reported that Spanish-speaking union represented workers were being taken advantage of, I knew I had to go down and see it for myself.

The post CALIFORNIA: Union hubris in any language appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] A Child Welfare Blind Spot: Proposed CT Bill Raises Interstate Protection Concerns
item.description:
A bill advanced by the Judiciary Committee is drawing concern from an unexpected source — the state’s own Judicial Branch, which warns a key provision could disrupt how states coordinate […]

The post A Child Welfare Blind Spot: Proposed CT Bill Raises Interstate Protection Concerns  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] New BLS Employment Data Revisions Reveal Massachusetts’ Anemic Private Sector Employment Growth Since 2020
item.description:
Massachusetts lost 35,000 private-sector jobs and is one of just six states yet to regain pre-pandemic employment levels  Boston – Data released today from the Bureau of Labor Statistics confirm that the Commonwealth has lost even more private sector jobs in the years following the COVID-19 pandemic than previously understood.  Pioneer Institute’s March 2025 report Massachusetts at Risk: The Alarming...

The post New BLS Employment Data Revisions Reveal Massachusetts’ Anemic Private Sector Employment Growth Since 2020  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Floating Offshore Wind – A Financial and Environmental Catastrophe
item.description:
Earlier this year, the California Coastal Commission released a report titled “Statewide Strategy for the Coexistence of California Fishing Communities and Offshore Wind Energy.” In addition to providing a “guiding framework” to protect California’s fishing communities, it “presents a roadmap for proposed offshore wind projects to become consistent with California’s relevant Coastal Act policies that recognize...

The post Floating Offshore Wind – A Financial and Environmental Catastrophe first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Idaho’s Budget Grew 2.5% Despite the Cuts, because of Welfare Spending
item.description: If you were paying attention to the budget debates on both the House and Senate floors during the legislative session, you might think the Legislature made significant cuts to the Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27) budget. Unfortunately, based on preliminary numbers, total appropriations are a 2.5% increase, with FY27’s $14.453 billion outpacing the FY26 original appropriation […]

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Feeding Our Future, sentence #10, over three years
item.description:
Abdullahe Nur Jesow was sentenced to 43 months in federal prison, becoming just the tenth defendant sentenced in the case, to date. That works out to 3 years and seven months. He was also ordered to forfeit $866,000. Jesow, now age 65, is defendant No. 28 (of 79) in the sprawling free feed scandal. He...

The post Feeding Our Future, sentence #10, over three years appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Jbartlett] SNAP candy & soft drink ban would hurt retailers
item.description:
Removing candy and soft drinks from New Hampshire’s SNAP program would cost retailers an estimated $5.3 million-$17.8 million a year in lost candy and soft drink sales alone, a new study by the Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy finds. The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services and the U.S. Department of Agriculture have […]

The post SNAP candy & soft drink ban would hurt retailers appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] New PRI Study Finds California Job Growth Lags Nation, High Costs Turn State’s Income Advantage into 35% Deficit
item.description:
A new study released today by the Pacific Research Institute finds that California’s economic performance has fallen sharply behind the rest of the nation, with job growth since the COVID-19 pandemic at less than half the national rate, while the state’s high cost of living is erasing its income advantage. Click here to download the […]

The post New PRI Study Finds California Job Growth Lags Nation, High Costs Turn State’s Income Advantage into 35% Deficit appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] The state of Whitmer’s state
item.description: Governor should use her remaining time to fix housing and literacy

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Infill rules help, but growth boundaries remain a housing obstacle
item.description:
The “landmark” measure — imposed at the behest of environmentalists and agricultural interests — was designed to stop urban sprawl, protect open space and promote transit use. “Observing the loss of farmland and greenspaces, as well as poorly planned development in other states, Oregonians saw that they could no longer take for granted the state’s […]

The post Infill rules help, but growth boundaries remain a housing obstacle appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Retired state employee backs pension buyouts for Chicago police, fire
item.description:
Even though some of John VanVleet’s peers were skeptical about the state’s pension buyout program for public employees, he decided to take advantage of it. “A lot of people had this conversation where they weren’t sure if they wanted to take it,” said VanVleet, 53, who was the training coordinator at Hill Correctional Center in...

The post Retired state employee backs pension buyouts for Chicago police, fire appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Albany Should Listen to Jamie Dimon
item.description:
In his annual message to shareholders, JP Morgan Chase’s chief executive, Jamie Dimon, offered a timely and pointed warning for....

The post Albany Should Listen to Jamie Dimon appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.
